content: Current research frames Self-Refine as a structural framework for iterative improvement and LLM self-reflection mechanisms as the internal perspective-taking process for generating critical feedback, so we can embed multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into the unstructured iterative feedback loop of Self-Refine to create a new zero-training framework that solves Self-Refine's blind spot of one-dimensional feedback. This modified framework would retain Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data while fixing the common problem of local optimization caused by narrow, consistent self-feedback.
